ogTitle: Cardigan vs Cardigan Village: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Cardigan and Cardigan Village.
heading: Cardigan vs Cardigan Village — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Cardigan, Victoria and Cardigan Village, Victoria. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Cardigan has a lower overall climate risk score (32/100) compared to Cardigan Village (36/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Cardigan or Cardigan Village safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Cardigan has a flood risk score of 53/100, while Cardigan Village scores 56/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Cardigan scores 20/100 for bushfire risk. Cardigan Village scores 44/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Cardigan or Cardigan Village?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Cardigan has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.92% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
